title: mariscus albosanguineus
intro: Mariscus albosanguineus, also known as the red-stemmed marsh marigold, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Mariscus albosanguineus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
